meta.title: Computer Science Basics
meta.description: Core concepts for the state exams

cards.question: What is the difference between a Process and a Thread?
cards.answer: A process is an executing instance of an application with its own memory space. A thread is a path of execution within a process that shares memory with other threads.

cards.question: What is a Deadlock in database transactions?
cards.answer: A situation where two or more transactions are waiting for each other to release locks, resulting in neither transaction being able to proceed.
